content: Yeah, this week has been really tough. I’ve been staying late almost every night trying to keep up with all these projects, but it just feels like no matter how much I do, there’s always something else waiting. I keep thinking I’m just not cut out for this kind of pressure, and that makes me feel stuck and exhausted. Sometimes I skip lunch or breaks because I don’t want to fall behind, but then I feel guilty later. And honestly, talking to my team about some of the harder tasks seems easier if I can avoid it altogether. It’s like if I show anyone how overwhelmed I really am, they’ll see me as incompetent. I just don’t know how to stop falling apart like this.

I just couldn’t handle what my mom said at dinner. It felt like she was completely dismissing everything I’ve worked on, and I just wanted to disappear afterward. After I left early, I kept replaying it in my head all night—how I felt like I wasn’t good enough no matter what I did. It’s hard to believe anyone would actually want me around after things like that. I don’t know why I even try anymore—it just ends up making me feel worse. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not meant to fit in with them. I don’t think I can face another family gathering without crumbling. It’s just easier to stay out of the way and avoid the pain of feeling so unwanted.

I just couldn’t stay at the gathering last weekend. It felt like everyone was talking about my sibling, praising them for their work, but not once did anyone acknowledge mine. I kept thinking maybe I’m just not good enough for them to notice me. I ended up leaving early and stayed in my room all night, repeating to myself how useless I really am. It’s hard not to feel like I’m invisible when it seems like that’s what everyone thinks. But then I also didn’t want to bring up anything about my own life because I feared they’d see me as less important or disappointing. It just seemed safer not to risk the disappointment. I don’t know if I’ll ever feel truly seen or appreciated by my family.

Yeah, so last week at that party, I found myself constantly checking in on other people, making sure they were having fun and laughing when others were talking. I kept thinking if I didn’t seem engaged or if anyone seemed upset, they’d think I wasn’t worth being around. It just felt safer to be hyper-aware of what others needed rather than focusing on myself or what I wanted. And even when I laughed at jokes I didn’t really find funny, I did it anyway because I didn’t want to seem boring or unlikable. I just... I wanted to fit in and not cause any problems or draw too much attention to myself. It’s exhausting, but it’s hard to stop. Sometimes I wish I could just be myself without feeling like I have to be this person who pleases everyone all the time. But I’m scared if I do that, things will get worse or they’ll just leave me out completely. So, I stick with trying to make others comfortable, even if it means I’m uncomfortable most of the time.

Yeah, the gathering was... really hard for me. Everyone started talking about stuff from my past, and it just felt like no matter how much I try to keep things together, it all falls apart. I kept thinking, “This is going to end in me being alone again,” and it just made me really anxious. So, I ended up leaving early because I couldn’t handle staying with everyone feeling so off-kilter. And then I just sat there quietly, away from everyone else, trying to push through those feelings. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m stuck in this cycle where I feel overwhelmed and like I’m just not enough. I don’t know how to stop feeling so out of control. Maybe I just shouldn’t even try anymore.

I guess last week was pretty rough at the dinner. Everyone seemed to know exactly what they wanted me to do, like they could read my mind or something. I just kept quiet because… well, I didn’t want to cause any trouble or make things worse. It felt like if I said anything, it would just end in arguments or silence anyway. Sometimes I wonder if I even get to choose how I live or what I do. It’s frustrating, but I don’t really know how else to handle it. Maybe I should just keep to myself most of the time. But then I feel lonely and like I’m missing out on being heard. It’s hard to shake this feeling that I’m stuck, no matter what I do.

Yeah, during the family gathering, when they started arguing about that thing, I just kept quiet. I didn’t want to cause any trouble or make anyone upset, so I just nodded along and smiled, even though I wasn’t sure what I really believed. But afterward, I felt really anxious and guilty, like maybe I let them down or something. I just wanted to get away from everyone so I wouldn’t feel so out of place or scared of saying what I truly think. It’s exhausting trying to keep everything together, but I guess I’m afraid if I don’t, I’ll disappoint them or lose their love. I don’t know, it’s like I’m stuck somewhere between wanting to be true to myself and feeling like I have no choice but to hide who I am. That’s why I keep pushing back until it hurts. I just wish there was a way to be honest without losing everything. Sometimes it feels like I’m trapped in this endless cycle of pleasing everyone else so I don’t get left alone.

Well, the meeting was... tough. When they asked me for that extra project, I just felt like my head was spinning, like there was no way I could keep up with everything else. I didn’t say anything because, honestly, I wasn’t sure how to explain that I couldn’t handle it. Afterward, I just kept staring at my computer and tried to push through, hoping that somehow it would all make sense once I worked late. But I guess I’m still feeling pretty overwhelmed, stuck, you know? It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never enough.

Yeah, it’s been… kind of tough. Everyone there seems really comfortable and connected, and I just feel like I don’t fit in at all. I mostly stay quiet because I don’t want to draw attention to myself or sound stupid if I say something wrong. It’s like I’m this awkward observer instead of someone who belongs. But then I catch myself staring at some of them, trying to figure out what they’re thinking about me, and it just makes me feel worse. I guess I just worry they think I’m weird or not worth their time. It’s exhausting trying to push through these feelings and just hang around. Sometimes I wonder if maybe I shouldn’t just stop coming here. But I also feel like missing out on this could mean I’ll never find anyone who actually likes me. It’s a confusing mess inside me. 💔🤔

Yes, the meeting started, and right away, my mind went to thinking I wouldn’t do well leading this project. I just felt... overwhelmed, like maybe I’m not really cut out for this responsibility. I kept telling myself if I screw up even a little, everyone will know I’m weak. So, I found myself mostly quiet, not saying much because I didn’t want to risk looking incompetent. Afterward, I stayed late, double-checking everything again, hoping maybe if I review it enough, nothing will go wrong. It’s hard, but I just don’t want to seem like I don’t belong here.

I’ve been trying really hard not to say anything that might upset you, even when I disagree with what you’re saying. I just worry that if I push back, you’ll lose interest or think I’m not good enough. It’s exhausting, but I don’t want to risk losing what we have, so I keep going along with whatever you want. I guess I just don’t see how else I could be except by pleasing you. Sometimes it feels like I’m losing myself in all this, but I don’t know how to stop without causing a big fight or getting rejected again. I just want things to stay the same, no matter how unhappy I might be.

I’ve been feeling really stuck with that new project on top of everything else. It just feels like there’s no end in sight, and honestly, I’m scared I won’t get it done right. I keep telling myself if I tell anyone how overwhelmed I am, they’ll think less of me or that I’m lazy. So, I just push through and try not to let anyone notice how exhausted I am. Sometimes, I wish I could just stop pretending everything is okay and admit I need help, but I’m scared people would see me as weak.

Yeah, the dinner was really hard. I tried to talk about some stuff I’ve been working on, hoping maybe someone would acknowledge it, but it felt like no one was even listening. It just made me feel... insignificant, like whatever I say doesn’t matter to them. So, I ended up just shutting down and sitting alone because I didn’t want to keep pushing when it seemed like nobody wanted to be around me. I just kept thinking maybe I’m just not good enough for them to care about what I have to say. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I feel pretty hopeless after that. I don’t know how to stop feeling this way.

I guess last week at the dinner was tough, especially when everyone started talking about my job. I just felt like I was failing somehow, like I’m not doing things right. It made me kind of shut down after that because I didn’t want to make things worse or say something stupid. I didn’t really want to stick around or say much after that either—just wanted to get out of there. And then avoiding the next family gathering felt easier, even though I know I probably shouldn’t. It just seems safer not to get hurt like that again.

Yeah, the dinner was okay, I guess. But when I tried to join in with what everyone was talking about, I just felt kind of stuck, like no one really wanted to talk to me. I kept changing the subject to things that seemed popular, hoping maybe they’d like it, but then I laughed nervously at some jokes and realized they probably thought I was... off-putting. It just made me feel worse, like I was doing something wrong. So, I ended up checking my phone a lot and left early because staying felt overwhelming. I just don’t know how to fit in without making a fool of myself first. It’s exhausting.

Yeah, the news about the rent increase really threw me off. I just feel like this is just another sign that I’m not managing anything right, and maybe that’s why no one wants to stick around or help me out when things get tough. I’ve been avoiding calling anyone because I don’t want to have to explain what’s happening or deal with disappointment again. It’s just...so overwhelming, and honestly, I don’t see a way out. Sometimes I wish I could just disappear and stop trying so hard to keep up. But then I think about how everyone else seems to handle these things, and it just makes me feel even worse. It’s like I’m failing at everything—and that’s when I start doubting myself completely.

I just... I don’t know, I felt like I let everyone down again. After the argument, I kept thinking, maybe this is why nobody wants to stick around—because I always mess things up. It’s hard not to blame myself when things go wrong, especially when I’m the one who’s supposed to be keeping everything together. I just wanted to disappear and avoid any contact because talking about it all felt too painful, like I’d just confirm what I already feared—that I’m not good enough for anyone. And honestly, sitting in my room all night just made me feel even worse about myself. It’s like every time I fail, I want to shut down completely instead of facing the fallout. I guess I don’t really know how to move past these moments without feeling so overwhelmed and stuck. Sometimes it feels like there’s no way out of feeling this way about myself.

I just got that notice about the rent being overdue again, and honestly, it just hits me hard. I keep thinking maybe this time it's because I’m not doing enough—like I’m failing at something important. It makes me feel so worthless, like no one would want to help me or even look at me if they knew. I’ve been staying up late trying to figure out ways to pay it all off quickly, but I just keep feeling exhausted and stuck. Sometimes I skip meals so I don’t spend as much on snacks or coffee, but then I worry even less about starving. It’s exhausting and scary, and I don’t know how to stop feeling so overwhelmed.

When I first got assigned to lead that project, I just felt this knot in my stomach, like I knew right away it wasn’t going to go well. I kept thinking, “I’m going to mess this up like I always do,” and then adding on top of that, everyone will see how incapable I really am. I found myself putting off most of the tasks because I couldn’t shake the feeling that I’d embarrass myself if I tried too hard or made a mistake. Sometimes, I stayed late trying to fix things, but it only made me more exhausted and stressed, and honestly, I still felt behind. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never enough for others, and that just confirms what I’ve been believing about myself—like I’m a waste of space. I don’t know why I keep believing this, but it feels so real when I’m stuck like this. I guess I just want to prove to myself and maybe others that I’m capable, but every time I try, it ends up proving I’m not. It’s exhausting, and it makes me feel so alone in all of this.

Yeah, during the meeting, I felt really nervous and like everyone was expecting me to mess up. So, I just stayed quiet most of the time, hoping maybe no one would notice how unsure I felt. When they asked me for something, I didn’t know what to say, so I just gave short answers and tried to get out of the conversation as fast as I could. It’s hard not to feel like what I say is wrong or that people don’t care about what I have to offer. I guess I just don’t want to risk being laughed at or seen as useless. Sometimes it feels easier to just keep quiet than to face that fear.

When I’m at parties like this, my mind just races—every time someone tries to talk to me, I imagine them noticing how nervous I am and thinking maybe they’re just pretending to be nice because they feel obligated. I don’t want to say anything that could make things worse or feel like I’ve let them down. So, I usually end up shrinking into myself or finding some reason to leave early. It’s exhausting, but honestly, I just feel safer that way. Sometimes, I wonder if I’m just not cut out for this kind of social interaction at all. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but my fear always seems to get in the way. I guess I just don’t know how to shake off those uncomfortable feelings without pushing people away or disappointing them. It’s like there’s this invisible line I keep crossing, and each time I do, I lose a little bit of myself. I wish I could just be myself freely, without worrying so much about what others think or expect from me. But until I find a way to cope better with these overwhelming feelings, I’ll probably stick to avoiding the chaos.

I’ve been... kind of stuck with this notice about my rent, and then there’s this job interview coming up next week, and honestly, it just feels like everything’s falling apart. I keep thinking maybe if people knew about how tight things are, they’d see me as some kind of failure or just someone who can’t handle basic things on my own. It’s hard to focus on anything else when my mind keeps jumping to all the ways I might mess up or let everyone down. I just don’t want to make things worse by admitting everything’s slipping through my fingers. Sometimes, it feels safer to stay quiet and avoid anyone who might judge me differently. But then I’m scared I’ll miss opportunities because I’m hiding behind this mask of silence. It’s exhausting, and I don’t know how much longer I can keep going like this without losing what little bit of myself I still feel like having.

When I heard what my partner said earlier, my heart just sank. I couldn’t help but think, no matter what I do, I’m always going to mess things up, and this time is no exception. I feel so guilty for wanting to cancel the plans, like I’m being unreasonable or selfish. And then, those terrifying thoughts just rush in—like they’re already there, whispering that they’ll all leave me once they see the real me. It’s hard not to start worrying that maybe I really am too flawed to keep anyone around. I find myself replaying that moment over and over in my head, trying to figure out if I’m just imagining the tension or if it’s real. I keep checking in with him, asking if he’s upset with me, just to be sure he hasn’t lost his temper or something. But honestly, I don't think I have much else to lose by agreeing to the plans, even if I don’t feel well. It feels safer to just go through with it and hope it doesn’t blow up later. It’s exhausting, but I don’t see another way out right now.

I guess what happened was that we were arguing about where we wanted to go on vacation, and he kept saying I wasn’t making up my mind and that I was being too slow to decide. I felt so anxious and guilty right away, like maybe I’m just messing things up between us. I tried not to push back or say anything that might make it worse, so I just apologized over and over and changed the topic to avoid any trouble. After that, I couldn’t stop thinking about how I always mess things up, and it made me feel really sad and worthless. It’s hard not to believe that if I could just get better at being the one who makes decisions right, everything would be okay. I don’t know why I keep messing this up when I want so badly to do better. Honestly, sometimes it feels like I’m stuck in this cycle of trying and failing. I just wish I could stop feeling so anxious and stop worrying so much about disappointing him. I don’t want to push him away, but I also don’t know how to be myself without causing problems. It’s exhausting, and I’m starting to doubt if I’ll ever truly feel like I belong in a relationship. What do you think I should do? How can I stop feeling so overwhelmed and start believing in myself again?

I guess I felt pretty stuck when my coworker asked me to join that new hobby group. Part of me wanted to say yes, but then I just kept thinking maybe I’d mess up and everyone would see how bad I really am at these things. It’s like if I show up, people will know right away that I’m not someone they should hang out with. So, I told them I couldn’t come, hoping maybe that would protect me from feeling embarrassed. But after that, I just ended up staying home and replayed all the times I’ve failed before, and it made me feel even worse about myself. I don’t know, it’s hard to shake this feeling that I’m always going to be on the outside looking in.

When the meeting was announced, I immediately felt this knot in my stomach, like I knew right away that I wasn’t going to be able to get through it properly. I just kept thinking, “What if everyone sees how incompetent I really am?” So, instead of trying to prepare, I told myself I’d rather just avoid it altogether. In the end, when I stood there with nothing to say, I felt more ashamed than anything—I kept apologizing and tried to explain away why it went so poorly. It’s like I was already convinced I couldn’t do it, and then I proved myself wrong somehow. I hate feeling like I’m relying on others so much, but sometimes it feels safer than facing my own limitations. Honestly, just thinking about it makes me feel kind of... sad and embarrassed.

Yeah, I got that notice about the overdraft again, and honestly, it just feels like no matter what I do, I mess things up. I’ve been avoiding looking at my phone since then because I don’t want to face the worry or the shame. It’s like everyone else seems to handle money better than me, and I just end up falling behind and letting everyone down. I don’t know how I’m going to pay those bills without getting stuck in this cycle again. Sometimes it just feels easier to stay quiet and hope no one notices I’m struggling. But then I feel left out when I try to reach out, and that makes me feel even worse. It’s hard to believe anyone wants to be around someone who can’t seem to keep things together.

I don’t know... When he suggested we spend the holiday with his family, I just felt this knot in my stomach like something bad was going to happen. It’s like every time I open up to someone, they just pull away or leave. I keep thinking that’s how it always goes—people only stick around until they can leave or prove they’re not worth staying for. I just wanted to run away after that, cry myself to sleep, because it feels easier than facing the truth again. But then, part of me wonders if maybe I’m just being too hard on myself—that maybe if I wasn’t so cautious and trusting less, things might be different. It’s exhausting, honestly. I don’t know how to stop feeling this way without just giving up. Maybe ending it now is the best thing for both of us. But then again, that thought makes me feel even worse because I still believe I’m the one who’s letting them down. It’s just so confusing. How do I know what’s really best?

I didn’t really feel like I was part of the conversation at the gathering. It just felt like everyone was talking around me, like I wasn’t important or interesting enough. I tried to say something a few times, but it didn’t seem to help much. After that, I just wanted to get away and be alone. I don’t know, it just made me feel... small and like maybe I shouldn’t even try anymore. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not supposed to be there or what I did wrong. It’s hard not to think that maybe they all just find me annoying or unlovable. I guess I just don’t want to make things worse by pushing too hard. I don’t know how to stop feeling so invisible. Maybe avoiding them is the only way to protect myself from more pain.

I just woke up feeling like no matter what I do, it’s never enough for anyone, especially my family. When they started criticizing me at the gathering, I just wanted to disappear and wasn’t sure how to keep going. So, I left early and stayed quiet afterward, not even answering my phone because I didn’t want to face them again. I’ve been avoiding food and washing myself less because I just don’t feel like taking care of myself right now. It’s exhausting trying to keep up with everyone’s expectations when I feel so stuck and like I’m failing. I guess I just don’t know how to stop feeling this way.

Yeah, so at the family gathering, when my kids started arguing like that, I just felt completely overwhelmed and like everyone was looking at me like I wasn’t doing enough. I kept thinking, “This is why they always say I’m a bad mom,” and it made me want to just shut down and get out of there. I ended up going to my room because I didn’t want to face anyone else after that. It’s hard not to feel ashamed when people compare you to your sister, especially when you realize you can’t control what your kids do. I guess I just worry that if I admit how much I struggle, everyone will think less of me. It’s exhausting trying to keep everything together and make sure no one gets upset. Sometimes it feels like I’m stuck between wanting to be there and running away from all of it.

I keep telling myself that if I don’t do everything perfectly, people will think less of me or blame me for failing. But then I just feel like I’m drowning under all this pressure and it’s exhausting. I don’t know why I keep agreeing to take on more when I know it’s too much, but I feel stuck and scared if I say no. Sometimes I wish I could just stop trying so hard and let things slip, but then I worry they’ll see me as lazy or irresponsible. It’s like I’m caught between wanting to prove myself and fearing that any mistake will make me look worse. I just hate feeling so overwhelmed and helpless all the time.

I just keep thinking about that bill again and it feels like another proof that I’m just failing at everything. I don’t know how to stop feeling so ashamed, like everyone else should be able to handle things better than me. I’ve been avoiding calling anyone because talking about it might make things worse, so I just keep scrolling through numbers instead. It’s exhausting, and sometimes I just want to stop eating because I don’t want to waste more money. Honestly, I don’t know what else to do except keep pushing myself, even if it means missing out on stuff I really want. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just messing up somehow. 😢

Yeah, the rent increase just hit me hard. I keep thinking that this proves I can’t handle even basic things like paying rent, and it’s making me feel really stuck and hopeless. I’ve been avoiding calling anyone because I don’t want them to see how overwhelmed I am or judge me for struggling like this. Sometimes I just lie awake worrying, wondering if I’ll have to move somewhere cheaper or lose my place. It feels like no matter what I do, I’m failing somehow. Honestly, it’s exhausting and kind of scary. I just wish there was something I could do right now that doesn’t make everything worse.

Yeah, so, um, this week… my partner brought up the idea of moving to another city for work, and I kind of just nodded along like everything was fine. I felt really anxious because I didn’t want to upset them, but part of me also felt frustrated because I was thinking, “If I say no, they’ll probably get mad or even leave.” So, I ended up trying to help with planning, even though I wasn’t really excited about it, just to keep things smooth. It’s frustrating because I want to be supportive, but I also feel like I can’t be myself. I guess I just worry that if I express what I truly feel, it’ll make things worse. That’s why I mostly try to hide how I’m really feeling. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to handle it.

When thinking about this date coming up, I feel this knot in my stomach, like I’m already on edge before it even happens. I keep telling myself if I mess up—even just a little—I’ll confirm everyone’s worst fears—that they won’t want me around. It’s exhausting, but I find myself double-checking every detail, trying to make sure I won’t say something wrong. And then there’s this sinking feeling that no matter what I do, I’m just going to disappoint them again, which makes me want to pull back and maybe cancel right away. It feels safer that way, even if it means missing out on what could be a meaningful connection. I just... don’t know how to stop feeling so scared and anxious all the time.

I’ve been stuck again at work this week, trying to keep up with all the deadlines, but it just feels like no matter what I do, it’s not enough. I keep thinking maybe I shouldn’t even bother trying—like I’m just stuck here, no matter how hard I try. So, I’ve been putting things off and avoiding talking to anyone at lunch because I don’t want to add to my frustration. I guess watching TV seems easier than dealing with the stress of everything else right now. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t see how things could get better unless something changes. But I also worry that if I push too hard, I’ll just mess things up even more. It’s like there’s this invisible wall preventing me from moving forward, and I don’t know how to break through it.

Yeah, at the event, I noticed a lot of people getting together right away, and I just felt left out. I kept thinking maybe I’m just not interesting or that no one wants to talk to me like that. It made me want to stay quiet and just watch because trying to join seemed risky. I ended up leaving early because staying felt too uncomfortable. It just reinforced what I’ve been feeling—that I don’t really belong anywhere. Sometimes I wish I could just disappear after these kinds of situations.

Yeah, the meeting was... tough. I tried to say something about the plans, but no one really seemed to listen. It just felt like whatever I said didn’t matter at all, like I wasn’t even there. And then, after that, I just kind of shut down inside—I couldn’t bring myself to say another word or even look at anyone. I ended up going to my room later because it just felt easier to keep to myself than to face that frustration again. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m stuck feeling like I don’t matter in this family.

Yeah, the dinner was... rough. When they started making plans, and I had something to say, it just felt like no one wanted to hear me. Like, maybe they didn’t even notice me there, or maybe they really don’t care about what I think. I kept quiet after that because I didn’t want to make things worse or seem like I was bothering them again. And then, later, I just couldn’t shake those thoughts—like maybe I’m just not lovable anyway. It’s frustrating because I want to be part of things, but every time I try, it seems like I’m just proving how invisible I am. I guess I just ended up feeling really alone and upset, like nothing I do matters. I didn’t want to ruin the evening so I left early, hoping maybe no one would notice I wasn’t feeling well. But now, lying here, I’m just replaying everything in my head and it hurts so much. I don’t know why I keep trying if it always ends this way.

Yeah, the party was... kind of overwhelming at first. Everyone seemed to be talking so freely, and I just kept feeling like I was out of place, like I was the odd one there. I found myself looking at my phone a lot, trying to avoid catching anyone’s eye, because I didn’t want to seem too interested or maybe even annoying. And when someone tried to talk to me, I just laughed along, but inside I felt like maybe they were thinking the same thing—like I was boring or awkward. By the time I left early, I was already convinced I shouldn’t have even bothered showing up. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but somehow I always end up on the sidelines.

Yeah, so, last week was really tough. My partner made a decision about where to go on vacation without asking me, and I just kept quiet because I felt like if I said anything, it would just cause an argument. I kept thinking maybe if I just agree and don’t push back, everything will be okay. But inside, I felt really stuck and frustrated—I just wanted to have some say in what we do together. Sometimes it feels like I’m not even part of the team when things aren’t going my way. I guess I just want to make sure I’m not messing up somehow. It’s frustrating because I don’t know how to stand up for myself without making things worse. I’m scared if I try to change things, I’ll just end up disappointing both of us. So, I stick to being the quiet one, hoping that if I stay quiet long enough, things will work themselves out. It’s exhausting, but I don’t really see another way. I just want to feel like I belong in this relationship, like I’m supposed to have a say. But it seems like no matter what I do, I’m stuck trying to please everyone else first. That’s what’s really hurting me right now.

Well, this week has been… quite stressful, honestly. Being in charge of this new project has made me really anxious because I keep thinking that if I make even one mistake, it’ll just prove how incompetent I really am, and everyone will see the worst of me. I find myself putting off important decisions because I’m afraid of messing up, and I keep worrying that if I give anyone else a chance, they’ll also fail. It’s exhausting, and I feel stuck between wanting to do well and avoiding the inevitable embarrassment that comes with showing my true nature. Sometimes, I just want to hide away from all this pressure and the constant scrutiny. But then I feel guilty for not trying harder, so I push through, only to end up more stressed. It’s like a never-ending cycle where I’m convinced I’m already failing before I even start. I don’t know how to stop this spiral without risking exposing something I don’t want anyone to see. That thought alone makes me feel so ashamed and overwhelmed. It’s exhausting trying to balance wanting to be good and accepting that maybe I’m just not cut out for this role. I wish there was a way to calm down enough to face it all without letting my fear control me. But every time I try to relax, my mind just snaps back to those same terrifying thoughts. It’s almost like my brain is programmed to find reasons to doubt myself and to be scared of failure. I just don’t understand why I can’t seem to escape this pattern. It feels like no matter what I do, I’m destined to be this toxic person everyone dreads. I hate feeling this way, but sometimes I wonder if maybe I am, and there’s no way to change that either. It’s overwhelming, and it’s hard to see a path forward when I’m so consumed by these doubts and fears. I wish I could just forget about all the

I just looked at the bank notification again, and my stomach sank. It feels like no matter what I do, this debt just keeps growing bigger and more complicated. I keep telling myself I’m just not cut out for managing this stuff—like I’m some kind of loser who can’t handle responsibility.
